evals.prompt: Review this PowerShell function for defensive coding anti-patterns. Focus on redundant guards, error handling, and control flow.
evals.expected_output: Should flag: (1) Test-Path before Remove-Item -Force is redundant, (2) Write-Warning + return instead of throw is warn-and-continue, (3) catch block that only logs and re-throws without adding context is useless
expectations: Flags the Test-Path check before Remove-Item -Force as redundant because -Force already handles non-existent paths
Flags the Write-Warning + return pattern as warn-and-continue — should throw instead
Flags the catch block that logs with Write-Error then re-throws as adding no value

evals.prompt: Review this PowerShell function for defensive coding issues. Look for validation patterns, return value design, and error signaling.
evals.expected_output: Should flag: (1) manual IsNullOrEmpty checks instead of ValidateNotNullOrEmpty parameter attributes, (2) bool return value pattern instead of throwing on failure
expectations: Flags the manual [string]::IsNullOrEmpty() checks and recommends [ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()] parameter attributes instead
Flags the return $true/$false pattern and explains the function should throw on failure rather than returning a boolean
